problem.title: Word Ladder
problem.description:
Given two words (beginWord and endWord), and a dictionary wordList, find the length of shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord.
                
Rules:

Only one letter can be changed at a time
Each transformed word must exist in the wordList
Return 0 if there is no such transformation sequence
assumptions: Each intermediate word must be in wordList.
Return length of shortest transformation sequence, or 0.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – BFS with Set [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use BFS to find the shortest path. Convert wordList to a set for O(1) lookup.

complexity.time: O(26 * L * N) where L is word length, N is wordList size

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Bidirectional BFS [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use BFS from both start and end to reduce search space significantly.

complexity.time: O(26 * L * N) but much faster in practice

solutions.title: Solution 3 – BFS with Word Pattern [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Pre-process wordList to create patterns for faster neighbor finding.

complexity.time: O(L² * N) for preprocessing, O(L * N) for BFS

solutions.title: Solution 4 – A* Search with Heuristic [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use A* search with Hamming distance as heuristic for better performance.
